span.div:
Calls query() each iteration
(loads the dev/unifier skill)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
gh pr create / view; git push
/ fetch / ff

span.div:
Has its remote on / PRs
opened against

span.div:
Runs forge serve / watch /
status / metrics in

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Writes the initiative
manifest (architect) +
verdict/feedback files

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
The single human running
forge across side projects.
Touches the system at exactly
three points: architect (idea
-> initiative), review
(engage the PR), reflection
(feedback).

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Anthropic's agent runtime +
harness (model calls, tool
use, prompt caching). Every
forge agent runs here; forge
hosts no model runtime,
vector DB, or harness of its
own.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Hosts the managed-project
repos. The PR is the
operator's sole review +
merge surface; merge state is
the single terminal signal.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
A side-project git repo forge
develops (trafficGame,
terraform-provider-betterado,
claude-harness...). Has its
own quality gate and its own
Brain 3. Worktrees branch
from it.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
External tree-sitter
knowledge-graph tool.
Rebuilds the three brain
graphs, triggered by a
post-commit hook.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Renders the brain as a
navigable vault so the human
reads the same graph the
agents query.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.div:
The operator's OWN Claude
Code session. The three human
moments run here as slash
commands (/forge-architect,
/forge-review,
/forge-reflect) - never
forge-spawned agents - and
hand off to forge via files.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Six phases backed by a brain;
designed to run primarily
unattended between three
human interaction points.
